1: Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
Yah comforts, three Isr. From nacham and Yahh; consolation of Jah; Nechemjah, the name of three Israelites.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Perhaps wait for Yah, father of Nehemiah. From the base of chakliyl and Yahh; darkness of Jah; Chakaljah, an Israelite.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
New moon, a month. From chadash; the new moon; by implication, a month.
The ninth month of the Jewish calendar. Probably of foreign origin; Kisleu, the 9th Heb. Month.
Twenty. From eser; twenty; also twentieth.
A year. (in plura or shanah; from shanah; a year.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
A castle, palace. Of foreign origin; a castle or palace.
Residence of Pers. kings. The same as shuwshan; Shushan, a place in Persia.
The name of several Isr. From chanan; gracious; Chanani, the name of six Israelites.
One. A numeral from 'achad; properly, united, i.e. One; or first.
A brother. A primitive word; a brother (like 'ab).
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Man, mankind. From 'anash; properly, a mortal; hence, a man in general.
From yadah; celebrated; Jehudah, the name of five Israelites; also of the tribe descended from the first, and of its territory.
To ask, inquire. Or shael; a primitive root; to inquire; by implication, to request; by extension, to demand.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
An escape. Or pletah; feminine of paliyt; deliverance; concretely, an escaped portion.
Jewish. Patronymically from Yhuwdah; a Jehudite, or descendant of Jehudah.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To remain, be left over. A primitive root; properly, to swell up, i.e. Be redundant.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
Exiled, captured, exile, booty. From shbuw; exiled; captured; as noun, exile; by extension, booty.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Probably foundation of peace, capital city of all Isr.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.

There, thither. A primitive particle; there then; often thither, or thence.
A province. From diyn; properly, a judgeship, i.e. Jurisdiction; by implication, a district; generally, a region.
Great. Or gadol; from gadal; great; hence, older; also insolent.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
A reproach. From charaph. contumely, disgrace, the pudenda.
A wall. Feminine active participle of an unused root apparently meaning to join; a wall of protection.
Probably foundation of peace, capital city of all Isr.
To break through. A primitive root; to break out.
A gate. From sha'ar in its original sense; an opening, i.e. Door or gate.
To kindle, burn. A primitive root; to burn or set on fire; figuratively, to desolate.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.

these were the ones who sealed the document:
